title: Use battle ID for local hax folder names instead of target name
description: Currently hax folders are created using the target name (e.g. ~/.synhax/my_target/). This causes conflicts when the same target is used in multiple battles. Change to use battle ID so each battle has its own isolated folder (e.g. ~/.synhax/battle-uuid/).

title: Prevent submissions after time stopped
Visual Diff Engine - Compare user output against target
description: Implement a visual diffing engine that compares user-rendered HTML/CSS output against the target image in real-time during battles. ## Reference Implementation https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wesbos/diff/refs/heads/main/scripts.ts ## Core Features 1. Capture user output (from AppFrame iframe) as an image 2. Compare pixel-by-pixel against target image 3. Generate visual diff showing where differences are 4. Calculate similarity score (0-100%) 5. Update score in real-time (every ~400ms) ## Key Technical Challenges ### Challenge 1: Capturing iframe content The user's code renders in an AppFrame (iframe with srcdoc). We need to capture this as an image. Options: - Use @zumer/snapdom to capture the iframe content - Use html2canvas on iframe document - Consider same-origin requirements ### Challenge 2: Target comparison format Currently targets are static images (`` tags). For pixel comparison: - Option A: Compare against the image directly (load into canvas) - Option B: If targets have code, render them in a hidden DOM element ### Challenge 3: Cross-origin considerations snapdom/html2canvas may have CORS issues with: - External images in user code - Fonts loaded from CDNs - The iframe sandboxing ### Challenge 4: Performance Running comparison every 400ms requires efficiency: - Scale down images before comparison - Debounce/throttle appropriately - Don't block the main thread ## Diff Algorithm (from reference) - Uses weighted pixel differences: R*0.299 + G*0.587 + B*0.114 + A*0.1 - Weights based on human eye sensitivity (green > red > blue) - Ignores very small diffs (<0.005) to handle anti-aliasing - Generates heatmap diff canvas using HSL color ramp - Returns score = (1 - avgDiff) * 100 ## Dependencies - @zumer/snapdom - For DOM-to-image capture - hslToRgb utility function (needs to be added)

title: Install and configure @zumer/snapdom dependency
description:
Add @zumer/snapdom package for DOM-to-image capture.

## Steps
1. Run: pnpm add @zumer/snapdom
2. Verify TypeScript types are available
3. Test basic capture in browser dev tools

## Reference
snapdom is used in the reference script to capture DOM elements as PNG images:
```ts
import { snapdom } from '@zumer/snapdom';
const snap = await snapdom(element);
const img = await snap.toPng();
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Package installed and importable
- Types working in IDE
- Basic test confirms capture works

title: Store diff score in hax table for live sync
description:
Persist diff scores to the database so they sync in real-time via Zero.

## Why This is Core (not future)
- Enables live spectator/referee views to see all participants' scores
- Score visible on producer dashboard during battle
- Enables 'end battle early on 100% accuracy' feature (battle_mode-1wt)
- Historical data for recap and leaderboards

## Step 1: Database Schema Change
File: src/db/schema.ts

Add to hax table:
```ts
// In hax table definition, add these fields:
diff_score: integer(),  // 0-100, null if not yet calculated
diff_score_updated_at: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }),
```

## Step 2: Run Database Migration
Push the schema changes to the database:
```bash
pnpm db:push
```

## Step 3: Regenerate Zero Schema
Generate the updated Zero schema from Drizzle:
```bash
pnpm sync-generate
# or: pnpm drizzle-zero generate --format -o ./src/sync/zero-schema.gen.ts
```

This updates `src/sync/zero-schema.gen.ts` (DO NOT edit manually).

## Step 4: Create Zero Mutation
File: src/lib/mutators.ts

```ts
updateDiffScore: {
  args: {
    haxId: 'string',
    score: 'number',
  },
  fn: async ({ args, tx, ctx }) => {
    // Verify user owns this hax
    const existing = await tx.query.hax.findFirst({
      where: eq(hax.id, args.haxId)
    });
    if (existing?.user_id !== ctx.userId) {
      throw new Error('Unauthorized');
    }
    
    await tx.update(hax)
      .set({ 
        diff_score: Math.round(args.score),
        diff_score_updated_at: new Date()
      })
      .where(eq(hax.id, args.haxId));
  }
}
```

## Step 5: Verify Zero Sync
- Start dev server: `pnpm dev`
- Start zero-cache: `pnpm sync`
- Verify the new fields sync correctly

## Update Frequency
Don't update on every comparison (too many writes). Options:
- Only update when score changes by >= 1%
- Throttle to max once per 2-3 seconds
- Update on significant milestones (crosses 50%, 80%, 90%, 95%, 100%)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] diff_score field added to hax table in schema.ts
- [ ] diff_score_updated_at field added to hax table
- [ ] Database migrated successfully (pnpm db:push)
- [ ] Zero schema regenerated (pnpm sync-generate)
- [ ] Mutation created in mutators.ts
- [ ] Score syncs to other clients in real-time
- [ ] Verified in dev environment

title: Create diff utility functions in src/utils/diff.ts
Create DiffEngine Svelte component for managing comparison state
description:
Create a Svelte 5 component that manages the diff comparison lifecycle and persists scores.

## File: src/lib/battle_mode/DiffEngine.svelte

## Purpose
Encapsulates the comparison logic, polling, state management, AND score persistence for real-time diffing.

## Props (using $props())
```ts
interface Props {
  contestantElement: HTMLElement | null;  // User's rendered output
  targetImageSrc: string;                  // Target image URL
  haxId: string;                           // For persisting score
  enabled?: boolean;                       // Enable/disable comparison
  interval?: number;                       // Comparison interval (default 400ms)
}
```

## State (using $state)
- score: number | null - Current similarity score (0-100)
- diffCanvas: HTMLCanvasElement | null - Visual diff output
- isComparing: boolean - Lock to prevent overlapping comparisons
- error: string | null - Any error message
- lastSavedScore: number | null - For throttling DB updates

## Behavior
1. When enabled and elements are available, start polling
2. Every interval ms:
   a. Capture contestant element using snapdom
   b. Load target image into canvas
   c. Run compareImages
   d. Update local score and diffCanvas state
   e. If score changed by >= 1%, persist to DB via mutation
3. Clean up interval on unmount ($effect cleanup)
4. Expose state to parent via bindable or snippet

## Score Persistence
```ts
import { z } from '$lib/zero.svelte';

// Throttled persistence - only save when score changes significantly
$effect(() => {
  if (score !== null && Math.abs(score - (lastSavedScore ?? 0)) >= 1) {
    z.mutate.updateDiffScore({ haxId, score: Math.round(score) });
    lastSavedScore = score;
  }
});
```

## Events/Callbacks
Consider exposing via props:
- onScore?: (score: number) => void
- onDiff?: (canvas: HTMLCanvasElement) => void

## Implementation Notes
- Use $effect for lifecycle management
- Debounce if contestant content changes rapidly
- Handle errors gracefully (CORS, element not ready)
- Consider using requestAnimationFrame for smoother updates
- Page Visibility API to pause when tab hidden

## Acceptance Criteria
- Component compiles with no TS errors
- Properly starts/stops polling based on enabled prop
- Exposes score and diffCanvas for parent consumption
- Persists score to database via Zero mutation
- Throttles DB writes appropriately
- Handles edge cases (null elements, failed captures)

title: Modify AppFrame to expose iframe content for capture
description:
The current AppFrame uses an iframe with srcdoc. We need to capture its rendered content.

## Current Implementation
File: src/lib/battle_mode/AppFrame.svelte
- Uses iframe with srcdoc containing combined HTML+CSS
- No reference to the iframe or its content is exposed

## Challenge
snapdom typically captures DOM elements, but iframe content is in a separate document.

## Approaches to investigate

### Approach A: Capture the iframe element directly
Some DOM capture libraries can screenshot iframe content.
Test if snapdom handles this automatically.

### Approach B: Access iframe.contentDocument
Since it's same-origin (srcdoc), we can access:
```ts
const doc = iframe.contentDocument;
const body = doc?.body;
// Then capture body
```

### Approach C: Use a different rendering approach
Instead of iframe, render in a shadow DOM or scoped div.
This would be a bigger change but might simplify capture.

### Approach D: Breakout page approach
The /battle/[id]/code/breakout route renders the code directly.
Could capture that in a hidden iframe and use its document.

## Implementation
1. Add bind:this to the iframe element
2. Expose the iframe reference to parent via bindable
3. Create helper function to get capturable element:
```ts
export function getCaptureElement(iframe: HTMLIFrameElement): HTMLElement | null {
  return iframe.contentDocument?.body ?? null;
}
```

## Testing
- Verify same-origin access works with srcdoc
- Test that captured element renders correctly
- Handle case where contentDocument is null

## Acceptance Criteria
- AppFrame exposes iframe reference
- Parent component can access iframe.contentDocument.body
- Works with the DiffEngine component

title: Create DiffPreview UI component
description:
Create a component to display the diff visualization and score.

## File: src/lib/battle_mode/DiffPreview.svelte

## IMPORTANT: Graffiti-First CSS
Before writing ANY scoped CSS, check https://graffiti-ui.com/llms.txt for:
- Existing utility classes (flex, grid, spacing, colors)
- Component patterns that can be reused
- CSS custom properties already defined

Only write scoped CSS for truly unique requirements that Graffiti doesn't cover.

## Design
Visual display showing:
1. Score badge (0-100%) with color coding
2. Diff canvas heatmap showing where differences are
3. Optional: side-by-side preview thumbnails

## Props
```ts
interface Props {
  score: number | null;
  diffCanvas: HTMLCanvasElement | null;
  contestantPreview?: HTMLImageElement;
  targetPreview?: HTMLImageElement;
  compact?: boolean;  // For sidebar vs modal view
}
```

## Score Display - Use Graffiti Patterns
- Look for existing badge/pill utilities in Graffiti
- Use Graffiti color utilities for states:
  - Score >= 80%: success/green state
  - Score 50-79%: warning/yellow state  
  - Score < 50%: error/red state
- Consider if there's a reusable ScoreBadge pattern needed elsewhere

## Layout - Graffiti Utilities
- Use flex/grid utilities for layout
- Use spacing scale (gap, padding) from Graffiti
- Use responsive utilities if needed

## Reusable Patterns to Consider
If these don't exist, consider creating them in Graffiti or a shared location:
- `.score-badge` - Could be reused in Header, Recap, Leaderboards
- `.canvas-preview` - For displaying canvas elements consistently
- `.diff-heatmap` - Specific to diff visualization

## Acceptance Criteria
- Displays score with appropriate color coding
- Shows diff canvas heatmap
- Handles null states gracefully
- Uses Graffiti utilities wherever possible
- Any new CSS patterns are reusable, not one-off

title: Integrate DiffEngine into BattleMode.svelte
description:
Wire up the diff engine into the battle mode UI.

## File: src/routes/(style)/(app)/battle/[id]/code/BattleMode.svelte

## Current Layout
- Splitpanes with left pane (App output) and right pane (Target image)
- AppFrame component renders user's HTML/CSS in iframe

## Integration Points

### 1. Get reference to AppFrame's iframe
```svelte
<AppFrame {hax} bind:iframeElement />
```

### 2. Add DiffEngine component
```svelte
<DiffEngine
  contestantElement={captureElement}
  targetImageSrc={battle?.target?.image}
  enabled={battleActive}
  bind:score={diffScore}
  bind:diffCanvas
/>
```

### 3. Display DiffPreview
Add to the layout - options:
- Below the target image
- In the header bar
- As a floating overlay
- In a new splitpane section

### 4. Conditional enabling
Only run diff when:
- Battle is active (between starts_at and ends_at)
- User has code to compare
- Tab is visible (consider Page Visibility API)

## State to manage
```ts
let iframeElement: HTMLIFrameElement | null = $state(null);
let diffScore: number | null = $state(null);
let diffCanvas: HTMLCanvasElement | null = $state(null);

const captureElement = $derived(iframeElement?.contentDocument?.body ?? null);
const battleActive = $derived(battle.status === 'ACTIVE');
```

## Layout considerations
The current layout uses svelte-splitpanes. Consider:
- Adding a third pane for diff preview
- Putting diff info in the header
- Toggle-able diff overlay

## Acceptance Criteria
- Diff score updates in real-time during active battle
- Diff preview visible somewhere in the UI
- No performance degradation
- Clean integration with existing components
notes:
## CSS Guidelines
- Always check Graffiti UI first before writing scoped CSS
- If creating a score badge component, make it reusable across Header, DiffPreview, and future Recap/Leaderboard views
- Keep BattleMode.svelte styling minimal - delegate visual patterns to child components

title: Handle target image CORS for canvas access
description:
Target images are stored in Cloudflare R2. Need to ensure they can be loaded into a canvas without CORS issues.

## The Problem
When loading an image into a canvas and then reading pixel data (getImageData), the canvas becomes 'tainted' if the image is cross-origin without proper CORS headers.

Error: 'The canvas has been tainted by cross-origin data'

## Current Setup
- Target images stored in Cloudflare R2
- Served via some URL (need to verify CORS headers)

## Solutions

### Option A: Ensure R2 bucket has proper CORS
Configure R2 bucket CORS rules to allow canvas access:
```json
{
  "AllowedOrigins": ["https://battlemode.dev", "http://localhost:*"],
  "AllowedMethods": ["GET"],
  "AllowedHeaders": ["*"]
}
```

### Option B: Use crossOrigin attribute
When loading images, set crossOrigin:
```ts
const img = new Image();
img.crossOrigin = 'anonymous';
img.src = targetUrl;
```

### Option C: Proxy through same-origin endpoint
Create an API route that proxies the image:
```
/api/target-image/[id]
```

## Implementation
1. Check current R2 CORS configuration
2. Test loading target image into canvas
3. If tainted, implement appropriate solution
4. Update loadImageAsCanvas utility to handle CORS

## Testing
- Test with production R2 URLs
- Test with localhost dev server
- Verify getImageData doesn't throw

## Acceptance Criteria
- Target images can be loaded and read pixel data
- Works in both dev and production environments

title: Performance optimization: throttle and scale for diff comparison
description:
Ensure the diff comparison runs efficiently without impacting battle UX.

## Performance Concerns

### 1. Comparison frequency
Reference uses 400ms interval. Need to verify this doesn't:
- Cause UI jank
- Slow down code editor responsiveness
- Drain battery on laptops

### 2. Image size
Large target images = more pixels to compare
Consider scaling down before comparison:
```ts
const scaleDownFactor = 2; // Compare at half resolution
```

### 3. Memory
Creating canvases every comparison could leak memory.
Reuse canvas elements where possible.

### 4. Tab visibility
Don't compare when tab is hidden:
```ts
document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', () => {
  if (document.hidden) pauseComparison();
  else resumeComparison();
});
```

## Implementation

### Throttle/Debounce
```ts
// Only compare after user stops typing for 200ms
// Then compare every 500ms while idle
```

### Scale optimization
```ts
interface DiffOptions {
  scaleDownFactor?: number; // 1 = full, 2 = half, etc.
}
```

### Canvas reuse
```ts
// Keep single diff canvas, clear and reuse
let diffCanvas: HTMLCanvasElement | null = null;
function getDiffCanvas(width: number, height: number) {
  if (!diffCanvas) diffCanvas = document.createElement('canvas');
  diffCanvas.width = width;
  diffCanvas.height = height;
  return diffCanvas;
}
```

### requestIdleCallback
Consider using requestIdleCallback for non-urgent comparisons.

## Metrics to track
- Time per comparison (aim for <50ms)
- Memory usage over time
- FPS during active comparison

## Acceptance Criteria
- Comparison runs smoothly without UI jank
- Memory stable over long battle sessions
- CPU usage reasonable

title: End timed match early when participant reaches 100% accuracy
description: When a participant achieves 100% accuracy in a timed battle, the match should end early rather than waiting for the timer to expire. This rewards perfect solutions and keeps the game moving. Implementation should: detect when any participant's hax reaches 100% match score, trigger battle end for all participants, and transition to the recap/voting phase.

title: Battle time extension setting - allow referee to extend or auto-end
description:
Add a battle setting that controls whether the referee can extend battle time after it expires.

## Behavior
- **If checked (allow_time_extension = true)**: Works like current behavior - battle stays in pending mode after time runs out until referee clicks 'End Battle'
- **If unchecked (allow_time_extension = false)**: Battle immediately transitions to recap screen when time expires

## Why
Currently all battles require manual referee intervention to end. This adds flexibility for:
- Casual battles that should auto-end
- Streamlined flow when ref is busy
- Solo practice sessions

## Scope
- Schema: Add boolean field to battle table
- UI: Checkbox in battle settings (lobby)
- Logic: Auto-redirect to recap when timer ends if setting is false
- Default: true (maintains current behavior)

title: Add allow_time_extension boolean field to battle schema
description:
Add a new boolean field to the battle table in Drizzle schema.

## File: src/db/schema.ts

## Changes
Add to battle table:
```ts
allow_time_extension: boolean('allow_time_extension').notNull().default(true)
```

## After schema change
1. Run `pnpm drizzle-zero generate --format` to regenerate Zero schema
2. Run `pnpm db:push` to push changes to database

## Acceptance Criteria
- Field added to battle table
- Default is true (maintains current behavior)
- Zero schema regenerated
- Database migrated

title: Auto-redirect to recap when battle ends if extension disabled
description:
When a battle's timer expires and allow_time_extension is false, automatically redirect all participants to the recap page.

## Files to modify
- src/routes/(style)/(app)/battle/[id]/code/BattleMode.svelte (or wherever timer logic lives)
- May need to check battle status handling

## Current Behavior
When timer reaches 0, battle stays in pending/waiting state until referee clicks 'End Battle'.

## New Behavior
When timer reaches 0:
- If allow_time_extension is true: Current behavior (wait for ref)
- If allow_time_extension is false: 
  1. Update battle status to ENDED (may need server action)
  2. Redirect all participants to /recap/[battleId]

## Implementation Notes
- Need to detect timer expiration
- Check battle.allow_time_extension value
- Either trigger mutation to end battle, or have server-side cron/check
- Navigate to recap page: goto(`/recap/${battle.id}`)

## Consideration
The redirect should happen for ALL participants in the battle simultaneously.
May need to use Zero's real-time sync - when battle status changes to ENDED, 
components should detect and redirect.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Battles with allow_time_extension=false auto-end when timer expires
- All participants redirected to recap
- Battles with allow_time_extension=true work as before
- No race conditions or double-redirects
